name: Alternative Strategies Index Tracker
reasoning: Product issued by Opus-Chartered Issuances SA (Luxembourg), classified under 'EAA Fund Alternative Other' category. 'Alternative Strategies' trackers typically invest in multi-strategy/fund-of-funds approaches that include options (hedge fund replication, tail hedging, volatility strategies). The Opus platform issues tracker certificates on actively managed indices that can employ derivatives including options. Limited public documentation available for the specific compartment/ISIN.
sources_checked: Opus-Chartered Issuances SA website, Morningstar category classification, Instifolio/Opus AMC 2.0 product documentation

name: Alternative Strategies Index Tracker_S2
reasoning: Series 2 version of the same Alternative Strategies Index Tracker from Opus-Chartered Issuances SA. Same structure and strategy approach. As an alternative strategies tracker, likely includes options as part of multi-strategy allocation.
sources_checked: Opus-Chartered Issuances SA website, Morningstar category classification, Instifolio/Opus documentation

name: Fidelity Alternative Bond ETF
reasoning: Fidelity Canada Alternative Mutual Fund (TSX: FFAB). The fund's simplified prospectus explicitly lists 'Options' as a permitted derivative for both hedging and non-hedging purposes. The fund strategy description mentions 'credit options' as part of its tactical fixed income toolkit. While the core strategy is long/short credit, options are a confirmed component of the fund's permitted and utilized instruments. Original judgment focused on holdings table not showing options, but the prospectus confirms options usage.
sources_checked: Fidelity.ca Alternative Bond Fund page, Fidelity Simplified Prospectus Part A (prospectus-fidelityfunds-part-a-en.pdf), Investcom fund data

name: Ironclad Absolute Aggressive Index Trkr
reasoning: Ironclad Absolute Aggressive Index (ISIN: DE000A4AJAD5) is a systematic, rules-based quantitative strategy generating buy/sell signals across market indices and equities. The methodology mentions allocations based on price activity, liquidity, technical data, and news flow. No evidence of options as a strategy component. Typical implementation for such systematic momentum/trend strategies is via futures and ETFs, not options.
sources_checked: Instifolio Ironclad Absolute Aggressive Index page, LIXX Index Administrator

name: Ironclad Clockwork Aggressive Index Trkr
reasoning: Ironclad Clockwork Aggressive Index (ISIN: DE000A4AJAE3) is a systematic quantitative strategy using proprietary algorithms for allocation decisions. Similar to Absolute Aggressive, it generates buy/sell signals without specific reference to options. The strategy focuses on equity indices and cash/money market instruments. No evidence of options usage.
sources_checked: Instifolio Ironclad Clockwork Aggressive Index page, LIXX Index Administrator

name: Ironclad Commodity Enhancement Stgy Trkr
reasoning: Ironclad Commodity Enhancement Strategy Index (ISIN: DE000A4AGEA9) uses a systematic quantitative algorithm for allocation across market components. The related COMMOENH index tracks 24 commodity futures contracts from the Bloomberg Commodity Index family. The strategy's commodity exposure is achieved through futures contracts, not options. No evidence of options being used in the strategy.
sources_checked: Instifolio Ironclad Commodity Enhancement Strategy Index page, COMMOENH Methodology (compassft.com), LIXX

name: Ironclad Diversified Armour Stgy Tracker
reasoning: Ironclad Asset Management's Diversified Armour strategy explicitly uses 'option-based tail risk hedging' as a core component. The strategy combines put option purchases for tail risk protection (catching fast market crashes) with trend-following (handling slow declines). Academic paper co-authored by Ironclad's Bruno Schwalbach details the options overlay methodology. Options are a confirmed core strategy component.
sources_checked: Ironcladam.com, Verum Capital GEO Global Armour factsheet, Investment Analysts Journal 2023 paper 'Leveraging defence into offence'

name: Ironclad Equity Armour Strategy Tracker
reasoning: Ironclad Asset Management's Equity Armour strategy uses explicit put option purchases for tail risk hedging. The strategy is part of the same Armour series as Diversified Armour, both using option-based protections combined with trend-following overlays. Academic research and fund documentation confirm options as a core component for equity protection.
sources_checked: Verum Capital GEO Global Armour factsheet, Investment Analysts Journal 2023, Ironclad Asset Management website

name: Ironclad Fortress Tracker
reasoning: Ironclad Fortress strategy described as maintaining global equity passive investment with trend-following and tail risk hedging overlays. Consistent with Ironclad's established approach across their Armour and Fortress product lines. Tail risk hedging typically uses put options. While a specific Fortress factsheet was not located, the strategy pattern aligns with Ironclad's documented options-based hedging methodology.
sources_checked: Ironclad Asset Management website, Ironclad research publications on tail risk hedging

name: Ironclad Risk Parity 15% Volatility Trkr
reasoning: Ironclad Risk Parity (15% Volatility) Index follows a standard risk parity methodology allocating across global equities, commodities (via futures), US Treasuries (via 10-Year and Ultra Treasury Bond futures), and TIPS (via PIMCO LTPZ ETF). Exposure is achieved through futures contracts and ETFs. The Solactive fact sheet and S&P Risk Parity methodology confirm futures-based implementation. No options identified in the strategy.
sources_checked: Solactive Advanced Research Risk Parity Index fact sheet (DE000SLA9Q71), S&P Risk Parity Indices methodology, Ironclad/Instifolio product page

name: KIS S&P500 VIX S/T Futures ETN(H)
reasoning: Korean Investment Securities ETN tracking the S&P 500 VIX Short-Term Futures Index. The index holds a rolling long position in 1st and 2nd month VIX futures contracts only. S&P DJI methodology confirms the index is purely futures-based with no options component. The VIX index itself is calculated from S&P 500 option prices, but the futures-based ETN does not hold or trade options.
sources_checked: S&P DJI VIX Futures Indices Methodology, KRX listing data, Barclays iPath VXX prospectus reference
